stream_a.title: EU countries to vote on CBAM carbon price deduction rules in September
stream_a.summary: EU member states will vote in September on rules defining which foreign CO2 prices importers can deduct from their Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) fees, a European Commission official said.

stream_a.title: Euro Markets: EUAs up 0.4% on the week as market settles in for rangebound trading ahead of reforms
stream_a.summary: European carbon prices posted a 0.4% weekly gain, unwinding the previous week's 0.4% decline as EUAs continued to travel sideways in a narrow channel ahead of mid-July's EU ETS reform package, while UKAs dropped 1.4% on the week amid ebbing confidence in a rapid linking agreement.

stream_a.title: Wind energy industry urges EU ETS overhaul to prioritise industrial electrification
stream_a.summary: The European Commission should use its upcoming revision of the EU Emissions Trading System (ETS) to redirect billions of euros in carbon market revenues towards industrial electrification, trade association WindEurope has said.

stream_a.title: Five European Commission departments object to draft EU ETS reform
stream_a.summary: Five European Commission departments have issued negative opinions on a draft reform of the EU’s Emissions Trading System (EU ETS), EU officials told Carbon Pulse.

stream_b.title: UK engineered carbon removal credits could top 850 mln by 2050 -report
stream_b.summary: Total UK-based engineered carbon removal credit supply could reach up to 852 million by 2050 under a high-growth scenario, according to modelling commissioned by the British government.

stream_b.title: India proposes carbon intensity targets for iron, steel under ETS
stream_b.summary: India's environment ministry has proposed carbon intensity targets for iron and steel producers under its Carbon Credit Trading Scheme, the largest sector yet brought under the compliance mechanism.

stream_b.title: FEATURE: Corporate non-market environmental donations are picking up, driven by employees and business value -experts
stream_b.summary: Voluntary giving by companies to environmental outcomes outside of formalised markets is starting to pick up this year after a few years of lacklustre growth, with more willingness to go public on action, and for reasons of talent acquisition and business value, according to experts in the field.

stream_b.title: Asia‑Pacific’s CDR market to reach $911 million by 2035, report says
stream_b.summary: Asia‑Pacific’s carbon removal (CDR) market is projected to reach $911.43 million by 2035, up from $160 million in 2025, growing at a 19% annual rate from 2026 driven by demand from heavy industry, power, and transport under tightening carbon pricing rules and net zero targets.

stream_b.title: The Philippines launches first policy framework for nature-based solutions
stream_b.summary: The Philippines has launched its first-ever national nature-based solutions (NbS) policy framework and catalogue, as the Southeast Asian country seeks to unlock more investment.

stream_b.title: Carbon ratings agency deepens Japan expansion with new partnership
stream_b.summary: A carbon ratings agency on Friday partnered with a Japanese developer to launch an initiative to improve transparency around the quality of forest carbon credits.

stream_b.title: EU pressed to frontload ETS cash to jump‑start carbon removals
stream_b.summary: The EU should use €10-30 billion of frontloaded revenues from its Emissions Trading System (ETS) to support durable carbon removal (CDR) before 2035, as waiting until compliance demand emerges risks leaving Europe short of its 2040 and 2050 removals targets, according to a recent paper.

stream_b.title: Agriculture body seeks panel members to develop new livestock ACCU method
stream_b.summary: An Australian agriculture body is seeking expressions of interest to help it develop a new method it has been assigned by the government to create.

stream_b.title: INTERVIEW: Korean securities firm eyes potential in growing CORSIA market, secures its first procurement contract
stream_b.summary: A major securities firm in South Korea has facilitated the country's first CORSIA-eligible emission unit (EEU) purchase agreement for a domestic airline, signalling its strategic push to expand its presence in the emerging compliance market for the aviation industry, a manager at the company told Carbon Pulse.
